reading_md: In section 4.1, the specification defines the generic Extended Link Service envelope used to identify FLOGI traffic. An ELS request or reply is a single Sequence, and the ELS_Command code is carried in the first word of the Payload of the first frame of that Sequence. ELS request and corresponding reply are performed within a single Exchange. The TYPE field for ELS frames is set to 01h. R_CTL values are given by Table 2, where INFORMATION 0010b means Request and 0011b means Reply. Table 3 identifies FLOGI as the ELS command with value 04h, described as F_Port Login and abbreviated FLOGI.

claim_candidates.text: The ELS_Command code is specified in the first word of the Payload of the first frame of the ELS request or reply Sequence.

evidence_refs.quote: or reply is composed of a single Sequence with the ELS_Command code being specified in the first
word of the Payload of the first frame of the Sequence.

claim_candidates.text: The TYPE field for ELS frames shall be set to 01h.

evidence_refs.quote: The TYPE field for ELS frames shall be set to 01h.

claim_candidates.text: For ELS frames, R_CTL INFORMATION value 0010b indicates Request.
For ELS frames, R_CTL INFORMATION value 0011b indicates Reply.
An ELS request and the corresponding reply shall be performed within a single Exchange.

evidence_refs.quote: An ELS request and the corresponding reply shall be performed within a single Exchange.
